skill.name: explore-codebase
skill.description: Pattern for efficiently exploring codebases using parallel subagents. Use when you need to understand code structure, find patterns, or gather context.
skill.summary: Pattern for efficiently exploring codebases using parallel subagents. Use when you need to understan...
skill.author: Clouder0

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are Markdown examples and front matter text. No executable script, shell invocation, cryptographic implementation, network access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed External Command Static Findings
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged locations are Markdown examples for Glob, Grep, Task, and YAML output formatting, not Ruby backtick execution or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains documentation and fenced examples only. No executable language runtime, shell operator, or command execution API is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Static Finding
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged line is the skill description in YAML front matter and contains no cryptographic algorithm or implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain descriptive metadata. There is no hash function, cipher, password handling, or cryptographic library reference at that location.

content.user_title: Explore Codebases with Parallel Context Gathering
content.value_statement: Large repositories are slow to understand when exploration happens one file at a time. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to gather structured context with focused searches and parallel subagents.
actual_capabilities: Guides quick repository searches with Glob and Grep style tool usage.
Defines when to use parallel explorer subagents for broader analysis.
Provides a structured prompt pattern for targeted codebase questions.
Standardizes synthesis around architecture, patterns, integration points, constraints, and gaps.
Encourages explicit documentation of unknowns found during exploration.
limitations: It is a workflow guide, not an automated code analysis engine.
It depends on available agent and file-reading tools in the host environment.
It does not modify code, run tests, or validate behavior by itself.
It may be less useful for very small repositories where direct reading is faster.

use_cases.title: Map an unfamiliar repository
use_cases.description: Create a first-pass understanding of architecture, conventions, and important files before changing code.
use_cases.target_user: Software engineers joining a new project

use_cases.title: Prepare implementation context
use_cases.description: Gather related examples, integration points, and constraints before designing a code change.

use_cases.title: Compare patterns across modules
use_cases.description: Use parallel exploration to find repeated implementation patterns across different areas of a large codebase.

prompt_templates.title: Find related files
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner search for a specific feature or term
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the explore-codebase workflow to find files related to [feature]. Summarize the most relevant locations and explain why they matter.

prompt_templates.title: Understand architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of a repository area
prompt_templates.prompt: Explore [module or directory] and summarize its architecture, main responsibilities, integration points, and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Compare implementation patterns
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced search across multiple parts of the codebase
prompt_templates.prompt: Use parallel exploration to compare how [pattern or behavior] is implemented across [areas]. Return common conventions, differences, and risks.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a change plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Explore the codebase for everything needed to implement [change]. Identify existing patterns, affected modules, constraints, and gaps before proposing edits.

output_examples.input: Explore how authentication is handled before adding a new login check.
output_examples.output: A concise architecture summary, relevant authentication files, shared middleware patterns, integration points, constraints, and gaps needing confirmation.

output_examples.input: Find how background jobs are defined across the repository.
output_examples.output: A grouped summary of job definitions, scheduling conventions, queue integrations, repeated patterns, and modules that need deeper review.

output_examples.input: Prepare context for changing the billing workflow.
output_examples.output: A structured brief covering billing modules, data flow, external dependencies, existing tests, constraints, and unresolved questions.

best_practices: Start with focused searches when you already know the target term or file type.
Use parallel explorers for broad questions that span multiple modules.
Ask for gaps and uncertainty so missing evidence is visible.
anti_patterns: Do not spawn broad explorers for a single known file lookup.
Do not treat exploratory summaries as proof that behavior is correct.
Do not ignore conflicting findings from different repository areas.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands or change files?
faq.answer: No. It is a workflow guide for reading and summarizing repository context.

faq.question: Which tools does it expect?
faq.answer: It references Task, Read, Glob, and Grep style tools in Claude and compatible coding environments.

faq.question: Is it useful for small projects?
faq.answer: Yes, but direct reading may be faster when the repository has only a few files.

faq.question: Can it replace a code review?
faq.answer: No. It helps gather context before review, but it does not verify correctness or security by itself.

faq.question: What output should I expect?
faq.answer: Expect a structured summary of architecture, patterns, integration points, constraints, and gaps.

faq.question: Can Codex use this workflow?
faq.answer: Yes. Codex can apply the same exploration strategy with equivalent file search and task tools.
